title: Cloud Photo Backup
drDigitalIntro:
Cloud photo backup keeps copies of your photos on the internet, safely stored by a company like Google, Apple, or Microsoft.

Why it matters:
• If your phone breaks, gets lost, or is stolen, your photos are still safe online.
• When you take a photo on your phone, it shows up on your computer too — that's syncing.
• "Synced" means the same photos appear on all your devices automatically.

How it works:
• Most photo apps (Google Photos, Apple Photos, OneDrive) offer cloud backup built in.
• You turn it on once, and it uploads every new photo over WiFi.
• Most services give you some free space (usually 5–15 GB), then charge a monthly fee for more.

The bottom line: turn on cloud backup and forget about it. If anything ever happens to your device, your memories are safe.
drDigitalSuccess: Cloud backup = peace of mind. Turn it on once and your photos are safe no matter what happens to your device.
drDigitalHint: Just read through and click Continue when ready.
